item.title: Southern giants set the pace as Grammar, Marist and Nasinu dominate Vodafone Deans Southern Zone quarter-finals
item.description: The three schools have emerged as the dominant forces heading into the Southern Zone quarter-finals, with each school qualifying with four teams across the various grades. Their strong representation highlights the depth of talent, preparation and commitment within their rugby programmes as they look to secure places in the Southern Zone semi-finals and eventually earn qualification for the Vodafo
item.content:encoded:
The three schools have emerged as the dominant forces heading into the Southern Zone quarter-finals, with each school qualifying with four teams across the various grades.

Their strong representation highlights the depth of talent, preparation and commitment within their rugby programmes as they look to secure places in the Southern Zone semi-finals and eventually earn qualification for the Vodafone Deans national competition.

Suva Grammar School once again demonstrated its consistency across the grades, with disciplined performances and a balanced squad.

Their success reflects the school's continued investment in developing players from the junior grades through to the senior divisions and their participation at the Suva Rugby Union club level.

Marist enters the knockout stage with four teams carrying the hopes of a passionate Marist community eager to see another successful Deans campaign.

Nasinu Secondary School has proven to be one of the biggest success stories of this year's Southern Zone competition matching the achievements of Grammar and Marist with four quarter-finalists. Nasinu has shown remarkable depth and resilience, setting up what promises to be an exciting knockout stage.

With all three schools boasting four teams each in the quarter-finals, they have established themselves as the benchmark for the Southern Zone.

Other prominent schools that will be playing in the quarter-finals include Gospel High School, Lomary Secondary School, and Lami High School.

The quarter-finals will be played at Grammar and Veiuto Primary School grounds with the opening game between Grammar and Marist in the Under 14 boys quarter-final at 8.30am.

The Vodafone Deans Southern Zone Under-18 competition will head straight into the semi-final stage next week, with only five schools participating in the top grade this year.

In the other quarter-final matches, the Under-15 grade will see Grammar take on Assemblies of God High School.

In the Under-16 grade, SGS will face Lami High School, while in the Under-17 grade, Grammar will play Nasinu.

In the Under-19 grade, Namosi Secondary School will meet AOG at 2:50 pm at the Grammar ground.

item.title: Nakasi High and Vunimono High set for U15 final showdown
item.description: Nakasi High School has booked its place in the Under-15 final of the Rewa Secondary Schools Football Association Competition after defeating Bhawani Dayal Arya College 2-0 in an impressive semi-final performance. Nakasi High School took the lead in the 21st minute through Roko Cinavilakeba, before Arnav Mani doubled the advantage in the second half to seal the victory.
item.content:encoded:
Nakasi High School has booked its place in the Under-15 final of the Rewa Secondary Schools Football Association Competition after defeating Bhawani Dayal Arya College 2-0 in an impressive semi-final performance.

Nakasi High School took the lead in the 21st minute through Roko Cinavilakeba, before Arnav Mani doubled the advantage in the second half to seal the victory.

In the first semi-final, Vunimono High School edged Nasinu Muslim College in a dramatic contest.

After neither side could break the deadlock in regulation time or extra time, the match was decided by a penalty shootout.

Vunimono held their nerve to claim a thrilling 5-4 victory and book their place in the final.

The stage is now set for an exciting Under-15 championship clash between Nakasi High School and Vunimono High School.

The Under-15 final will kick off at 9am tomorrow at Ratu Cakobau Park in Nausori, with supporters encouraged to turn out in large numbers to cheer on the young footballers as they compete for the title.

item.title: Justin Bieber joins Madonna, Shakira and BTS for FIFA World Cup final half-time show
item.description: Justin Bieber has joined the star-studded list of half-time performers who will close out the 2026 FIFA World Cup in New York.The

item.title: Fresh faces expected in All Blacks side to face Italy
item.description: The All Blacks are expected to make changes to their side for Saturday’s Test against Italy in the capital, with several players in line for an opportunity. Uncapped wing Josh Moorby and loose forward Anton Segner could be among those selected, with Segner on the verge of becoming the first German-born player to represent the All Blacks.
item.content:encoded:
The All Blacks are expected to make changes to their side for Saturday’s Test against Italy in the capital, with several players in line for an opportunity.

Uncapped wing Josh Moorby and loose forward Anton Segner could be among those selected, with Segner on the verge of becoming the first German-born player to represent the All Blacks.

Assistant coach Neil Barnes says some players who performed well last week may miss out, while others will be rewarded for strong performances.

Barnes says the All Blacks remain focused on winning the Test convincingly while also giving other players a chance to prove themselves.

New Zealand will play Italy at 5.05pm on Saturday at Sky Stadium.

In other games on Saturday , Australia will host France at 7.40pm while and Ireland will take on Japan at 10.10pm.

item.title: Egypt allege referee bias towards Argentina and Messi after dramatic World Cup exit
item.description: Egypt have alleged refereeing bias in favour of Argentina and Lionel Messi following their dramatic 3-2 loss in the FIFA World Cup round of 16. Egypt had led 2-0 with 11 minutes remaining and were closing in on a historic first-ever World Cup quarter-final appearance before Argentina staged a stunning comeback and scored the winner in stoppage time.
item.content:encoded:
Egypt have alleged refereeing bias in favour of Argentina and Lionel Messi following their dramatic 3-2 loss in the FIFA World Cup round of 16.

Egypt had led 2-0 with 11 minutes remaining and were closing in on a historic first-ever World Cup quarter-final appearance before Argentina staged a stunning comeback and scored the winner in stoppage time.

Egypt coach Hossam Hassan says his side was treated unfairly and suffered injustice.

Hassan has also questioned whether there was a desire to keep defending champions Argentina and Messi in the tournament.

He says perhaps they wanted to keep the world champions in the competition and perhaps they wanted Messi to stay in the running.

Egypt have blamed what they describe as serious refereeing mistakes and double standards from French referee Francois Letexier and his officiating team.

The controversy includes a disallowed goal for Egypt after a VAR intervention.

Mostafa Zico’s goal was ruled out after Marwan Attia was penalised for stepping on the foot of Argentina defender Lisandro Martinez earlier in the move.

Egypt also believed they had two possible penalty claims before Enzo Fernandez headed home Argentina’s winning goal.

Hamdi Fathy went down claiming he was held by Alexis Mac Allister, while Mohamed Salah beli eved he was tripped by Julian Alvarez.

However, replays were unclear on the Fathy incident, while the contact involving Salah was not considered sufficient for a penalty.

Egypt have reportedly asked FIFA to remove the officials involved in the match from the tournament.

Argentina, who also battled to a 3-2 win over Cape Verde in the previous knockout round, remain in contention to defend their World Cup title.

item.title: NSW take State of Origin series with 30-12 win over Queensland
item.description: New South Wales have produced a stunning 30-12 victory over Queensland at Suncorp Stadium to seal the 2026 State of Origin shield. After being outplayed in Sydney and heavily beaten in Melbourne, the Blues responded in the decider with a five-try performance to silence the home crowd.
item.content:encoded:
New South Wales have produced a stunning 30-12 victory over Queensland at Suncorp Stadium to seal the 2026 State of Origin shield.

After being outplayed in Sydney and heavily beaten in Melbourne, the Blues responded in the decider with a five-try performance to silence the home crowd.

Nathan Cleary led the way for NSW, scoring two tries and kicking five goals from five attempts.

Cleary was also awarded the Wally Lewis Medal as player of the series.

The Blues controlled much of the first half , with Cleary directing play while Mitchell Moses repeatedly pushed Queensland back with his kicking game.

Cleary opened the scoring after stepping past Kurt Capewell and powering through the Maroons defence.

NSW extended their lead after a strong attacking move involving Liam Martin, Stephen Crichton and Mark Nawaqanitawase ended with Cleary scoring his second try.

Cameron Murray then crossed to push the Blues further ahead as NSW took control of the contest.

Queensland responded through Hamiso Tabuai-Fidow after Cameron Munster created an opportunity late in the first half.

The match also saw NSW captain James Tedesco leave the field for a Head Injury Assessment following a collision with Briton Nikora just before half-time.

NSW held on to complete one of the major upsets in State of Origin history and secure the series with a 30-12 win .

item.title: British wildcard Fery storms into Wimbledon semi finals
item.description: British wildcard Arthur Fery has continued his sensational Wimbledon run with a straight-sets victory over world number 10 Flavio Cobolli to reach the semi finals. The world number 114 defeated the ninth seed 6-4, 7-6, 6-0 in front of an ecstatic Centre Court crowd.
item.content:encoded:
British wildcard Arthur Fery has continued his sensational Wimbledon run with a straight-sets victory over world number 10 Flavio Cobolli to reach the semi finals.

The world number 114 defeated the ninth seed 6-4, 7-6, 6-0 in front of an ecstatic Centre Court crowd.

The 23-year-old is the first men’s wildcard in 25 years to reach the Wimbledon semi finals and only the fifth British player in the Open era to make the men’s singles last four at the tournament.

Fery, who was ranked outside the world’s top 500 just 18 months ago, is now guaranteed to rise to world number 36 and become the new British number one.

The semi-finals will take place this Saturday, with Jannik Sinner facing Novak Djokovic and Fery playing Alexander Zverev.

The times for the semi finals are yet to be confirmed.

item.title: David Tolu Young named Fiji flag bearer, Anahira McCutcheon baton carrier for Commonwealth Games
item.description: Fijian swimming sensation David Tolu Young will be the flag bearer, while Anahira McCutcheon has been selected as the baton bearer

item.description: M-PAiSA has launched its first-ever sponsorship venture under Digital Financial Services Pte Limited, backing a nationwide Netball

item.title: Seruvakula calls for discipline ahead of England Test
item.description: FIJI Water Flying Fijians coach Senirusi Seruvakula says his side must be more disciplined in both attack and defence if they are

item.title: Nawaqanitawase to start for NSW Blues in Game 3
item.description: Mark Nawaqanitawase will line-up again for the NSW Blues when they face the Queensland Maroons in Game 3 of the State of Origin to

item.title: Switzerland beat Colombia on penalties 4-3 and will face Argentina in quarter-finals
item.description: Switzerland have booked a quarter-final clash against defending champions Argentina at the FIFA World Cup after beating Colombia 4

item.title: Eseta Waqaira replaces injured Kalesi Tawake in Fiji Pearls squad
item.description: Eseta Waqaira has been named as a replacement for Kalesi Tawake in the Fiji Pearls squad for the upcoming Spirit Series in Sydney.
